key_insight: HVAF spraying can produce thick NiTi coatings that retain martensitic transformation and functional shape-memory behavior after annealing, despite microstructural defects.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical route to wear- and damage-resistant smart coatings for components that need both surface protection and thermomechanical actuation.
method: HVAF thermal spraying of NiTi onto mild steel using four processing parameter sets
Characterization of composition, porosity, microstructure, and phase transformation behavior
Post-spray annealing followed by bending, nanoindentation, and scratch-based functional tests
evidence_signal: The coatings were evaluated by composition and microstructure analysis, phase-transformation measurements, and functional tests showing thermal actuation and shape-memory response.
watch_item: As-sprayed coatings are highly defective and need annealing; tensile strength remains limited, so processing control and substrate adhesion must be managed carefully.
paper_title: HVAF Spraying of NiTi Coatings: Microstructure, Phase Transformation and Shape Memory Behavior
